what_it_does: Stores a verbatim transcript of Tim's UI feedback on an early artefact, plus immediate follow-up instructions, as a noticeboard block intended for a client profile and for driving design changes.
observations: Verbatim oral-style feedback from a client/user named Tim
Complains the always-visible top banner is too big and should be a single strap with hamburger and short, untruncated title
Suggests tapping the top bar/title could expand it or open a document dropdown
Wants the Safari bottom bar hidden for home-screen saving via theme color, background match, and a V logo
Wants the 'comment on whole document' button converted to an icon in the top bar
Wants section-level comment controls as icon buttons placed within the section
Dislikes emojis and prefers the DNA system's brown icon-in-a-circle iconography
Finds the selected-content preview inside the comment panel redundant because the selection is already highlighted
Wants the comment input field to default to one line instead of a tall box
Wants Cancel/Post buttons turned into icon buttons and an added immediate-send icon
Requests this message be added to a file with his other feedback messages
Requests a new short-lived section for agent-related feedback and notes
Asks to look at document-switching logic and fix bugs/issues
Mentions agent outputs going into the noticeboard somewhere
standouts: Primary-source verbatim client feedback captured as a structured ledger item
Concrete, opinionated UI redesign instructions spanning chrome, iconography, and input controls
Explicit emotional/design preference ('I hate emojis') and reference to an existing 'DNA system' icon set
Meta-instructions to integrate the feedback into a client profile and to create a section for short-lived agent feedback
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps
Uses a board:// URI for self-reference and a part_of link to another board item
Body is Markdown with a level-1 heading and a verbatim transcript paragraph
concerns: Verbatim transcript contains disfluencies and likely transcription errors (e.g., 'second Demon', 'section 8')
Multiple distinct requests (UI changes, file organization, bug fixes, agent integration) are mixed in one block without segmentation
No explicit links to the artefact, screenshots, or the referenced 'file you've just made'
notes: A verbatim client feedback noticeboard item that records Tim's UI critique and follow-up instructions for the artefact and process.
questions: Which file is 'that file you've just made with my other ones'?
What are the exact icons/specs of the 'DNA system' brown icon-in-a-circle set?
Which V icon or logo should be used for the home-screen icon?
What specific bugs exist in the document-switching logic?
Where exactly should 'agent outputs' be routed in the noticeboard?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to capture verbatim UI feedback for a client profile; the content matches that and additionally includes implementation instructions and process requests.
apparent_themes: Contextual command surfaces (long-hold secondary menu, expandable top bar)
Iterative design driven by direct user critique
Integration of agent outputs into a feedback ledger
intention_signals: To preserve an authentic, unedited record of Tim's reaction and to turn his spontaneous comments into actionable UI changes and a new ephemeral feedback channel.
novelty: A raw, spoken-style transcript is filed as a first-class structured ledger item, blending emotional design preferences, concrete UI edits, and meta-process instructions in one block.
contribution: Contributes a primary-source client feedback record that specifies UI preferences (smaller banner, hidden Safari bottom bar, icon buttons, emoji removal, one-line input, immediate send), references an existing iconography system, and instructs process changes (client-profile filing, short-lived agent-feedback section, document-switch bug fixes, agent-output integration).
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard block containing Tim's verbatim UI feedback on an early artefact, requesting a smaller top banner, a hidden Safari bottom bar with matching theme color and logo, icon-based comment controls, removal of emoji, a one-line comment input, an immediate-send button, and a new short-lived section for agent-related feedback.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve Tim's raw UI critique and implementation instructions as a client-profile source and to direct subsequent design and code changes.
